concepts.name: SkillLibraryEntry
concepts.what: A single skill entry in a library catalog.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Skill name (e.g. `"ct-research-agent"`).

properties.name: description
properties.description: Human-readable description.

properties.name: version
properties.description: Semantic version string.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Relative path within the skills library.

properties.name: references
properties.description: File references used by the skill.

properties.name: core
properties.description: Whether this is a core skill.

properties.name: category
properties.description: Skill category tier.

properties.name: tier
properties.description: Numeric tier (0-3).

properties.name: protocol
properties.description: Associated protocol name, or `null`.

properties.name: dependencies
properties.description: Direct dependency skill names.

properties.name: sharedResources
properties.description: Shared resource names this skill uses.

properties.name: compatibility
properties.description: Compatible agent/context types.

properties.name: license
properties.description: SPDX license identifier.

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: Arbitrary metadata.

concepts.name: SkillLibraryValidationResult
concepts.what: Validation result from skill frontmatter validation.

properties.name: valid
properties.description: Whether the skill passed validation (no error-level issues).

properties.name: issues
properties.description: Individual validation issues.

concepts.name: ProviderStatus
concepts.what: Lifecycle status identifier stored in registry.json.

concepts.name: McpConfigFormat
concepts.what: Supported MCP config file formats.

concepts.name: HookEvent
concepts.what: Hook lifecycle event identifier from registry.json.

concepts.name: CtSkillEntry
concepts.what: Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryEntry`.

concepts.name: CtValidationResult
concepts.what: Backward-compatible alias for `SkillLibraryValidationResult`.

concepts.name: ConfigFormat
concepts.what: Supported configuration file formats.  - `"json"` - Standard JSON - `"jsonc"` - JSON with comments (comment-preserving via jsonc-parser) - `"yaml"` - YAML (via js-yaml) - `"toml"` - TOML (via iarna/toml)

concepts.name: DetectionConfig
concepts.what: Configuration for detecting whether a provider is installed.

properties.name: methods
properties.description: Detection methods to try, in order.

properties.name: binary
properties.description: Binary name to look up on PATH (for `"binary"` method).

properties.name: directories
properties.description: Directories to check for existence (for `"directory"` method).

properties.name: appBundle
properties.description: macOS .app bundle name (for `"appBundle"` method).

properties.name: flatpakId
properties.description: Flatpak application ID (for `"flatpak"` method).

concepts.name: McpServerConfig
concepts.what: Canonical MCP server configuration.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Transport type (`"stdio"`, `"sse"`, or `"http"`).

properties.name: url
properties.description: URL for remote MCP servers.

properties.name: headers
properties.description: HTTP headers for remote MCP servers.

properties.name: command
properties.description: Command to run for stdio MCP servers.

properties.name: args
properties.description: Arguments for the stdio command.

properties.name: env
properties.description: Environment variables for the stdio process.

concepts.name: SkillEntry
concepts.what: A discovered skill entry with its location and metadata.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Skill name.

properties.name: scopedName
properties.description: Scoped name (may include marketplace scope).

properties.name: path
properties.description: Absolute path to the skill directory.

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: Parsed SKILL.md frontmatter metadata.

properties.name: source
properties.description: Original source from which the skill was installed.

concepts.name: LockEntry
concepts.what: A single entry in the CAAMP lock file tracking an installed skill or MCP server.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Skill or server name.

properties.name: scopedName
properties.description: Scoped name (may include marketplace scope).

properties.name: source
properties.description: Original source string.

properties.name: sourceType
properties.description: Classified source type.

properties.name: version
properties.description: Version string or commit SHA.

properties.name: installedAt
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of first installation.

properties.name: updatedAt
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last update.

properties.name: agents
properties.description: Provider IDs this entry is linked to.

properties.name: canonicalPath
properties.description: Absolute path to canonical installation.

properties.name: isGlobal
properties.description: Whether this was installed globally.

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project directory (for project-scoped installs).

concepts.name: MarketplaceSearchResult
concepts.what: Paginated search results from a marketplace API.

properties.name: skills
properties.description: Array of matching skills.

properties.name: total
properties.description: Total number of matching results.

properties.name: limit
properties.description: Maximum results per page.

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Offset into the result set.

concepts.name: AuditResult
concepts.what: Aggregate audit result for a single file.

properties.name: file
properties.description: Path to the scanned file.

properties.name: findings
properties.description: All findings for this file.

properties.name: score
properties.description: Security score from 0 (dangerous) to 100 (clean).

properties.name: passed
properties.description: Whether the file passed the audit (no critical/high findings).

concepts.name: InjectionStatus
concepts.what: Status of a CAAMP injection block in an instruction file.  - `"current"` - Injection block exists and matches expected content - `"outdated"` - Injection block exists but content differs - `"missing"` - Instruction file does not exist - `"none"` - File exists but has no CAAMP injection block

concepts.name: InjectionCheckResult
concepts.what: Result of checking a single instruction file for CAAMP injection status.

properties.name: file
properties.description: Absolute path to the instruction file.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: Provider ID that owns this instruction file.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Current injection status.

properties.name: fileExists
properties.description: Whether the instruction file exists on disk.

concepts.name: McpServerEntry
concepts.what: An MCP server entry read from a provider's config file.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Server name (the key in the config file).

properties.name: providerId
properties.description: Provider ID that owns this config file.

properties.name: providerName
properties.description: Human-readable provider name.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Whether from project or global config.

properties.name: configPath
properties.description: Absolute path to the config file.

properties.name: config
properties.description: Raw server configuration object.

relatedTo: `McpServerConfig` for the canonical server configuration format.

concepts.name: GlobalOptions
concepts.what: Global CLI options shared across all CAAMP commands.

properties.name: agent
properties.description: Target agent IDs (repeatable).

properties.name: global
properties.description: Operate on global config instead of project.

properties.name: yes
properties.description: Skip confirmation prompts.

properties.name: all
properties.description: Target all detected agents.

properties.name: json
properties.description: Output as JSON.

properties.name: dryRun
properties.description: Preview changes without writing.

properties.name: verbose
properties.description: Enable debug logging.

properties.name: quiet
properties.description: Suppress non-error output.

concepts.name: EnsureProviderInstructionFileOptions
concepts.what: Options for ensuring a provider instruction file.

properties.name: references
properties.description: `\@` references to inject (e.g. `["\@AGENTS.md"]`).

properties.name: content
properties.description: Optional inline content blocks.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Whether this is a global or project-level file.

concepts.name: EnsureProviderInstructionFileResult
concepts.what: Result of ensuring a provider instruction file.

properties.name: filePath
properties.description: Absolute path to the instruction file.

properties.name: instructFile
properties.description: Instruction file name from the provider registry.

properties.name: action
properties.description: Action taken.

properties.name: providerId
properties.description: Provider ID.

concepts.name: DetectionResult
concepts.what: Result of detecting whether a provider is installed on the system.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: The provider that was checked.

properties.name: installed
properties.description: Whether the provider was detected as installed.

properties.name: methods
properties.description: Detection methods that matched (e.g. `["binary", "directory"]`).

properties.name: projectDetected
properties.description: Whether the provider has project-level config in the current directory.

concepts.name: DetectionCacheOptions
concepts.what: Options for controlling the detection result cache.

properties.name: forceRefresh
properties.description: Whether to bypass the cache and force a fresh detection scan.

properties.name: ttlMs
properties.description: Time-to-live for cached results in milliseconds.

concepts.name: SkillInstallResult
concepts.what: Result of installing a skill to the canonical location and linking to agents.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Skill name.

properties.name: canonicalPath
properties.description: Absolute path to the canonical installation directory.

properties.name: linkedAgents
properties.description: Provider IDs that were successfully linked.

properties.name: errors
properties.description: Error messages from failed link operations.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether at least one agent was successfully linked.

concepts.name: BatchInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options for rollback-capable batch installation.

properties.name: providers
properties.description: Explicit list of providers to target, auto-detected if omitted.

properties.name: minimumPriority
properties.description: Minimum provider priority threshold for filtering.

properties.name: skills
properties.description: Skill operations to apply in the batch.

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project root directory, defaults to `process.cwd()`.

concepts.name: BatchInstallResult
concepts.what: Result of rollback-capable batch installation.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether all operations completed successfully.

properties.name: providerIds
properties.description: IDs of providers that were targeted.

properties.name: skillsApplied
properties.description: Number of skill installations that were applied.

properties.name: rollbackPerformed
properties.description: Whether rollback was performed due to a failure.

properties.name: rollbackErrors
properties.description: Error messages from any failures during rollback.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Error message from the operation that triggered rollback.

concepts.name: ResolveTierDirOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `resolveTierDir`.

properties.name: tier
properties.description: Tier to resolve.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: Asset kind (extensions, prompts, themes, sessions, cant).

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project directory used when `tier` is `project`. Ignored for other tiers. When omitted with `tier='project'` the caller MUST substitute `process.cwd()` before invoking the resolver, so the harness never silently resolves to an unexpected working directory.

concepts.name: ResolveDefaultTargetProvidersOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `resolveDefaultTargetProviders` (defined in `./index.ts`).

properties.name: explicit
properties.description: Explicit list of providers requested by the user (e.g. via `--agent`).

concepts.name: ExtensionEntry
concepts.what: Metadata describing a Pi extension discovered on disk.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Extension name (file basename without the `.ts` extension).

properties.name: tier
properties.description: Tier at which this entry lives.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Absolute on-disk path to the extension file.

properties.name: shadowed
properties.description: When `true`, this entry is shadowed by a higher-precedence entry with the same name. Exposed so list output can warn about cross-tier name collisions per ADR-035 §D1.

concepts.name: PromptEntry
concepts.what: Metadata describing a Pi prompt directory discovered on disk.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Prompt name (directory basename).

properties.name: tier
properties.description: Tier at which this entry lives.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Absolute on-disk path to the prompt directory.

properties.name: shadowed
properties.description: See `ExtensionEntry.shadowed`.

concepts.name: ThemeEntry
concepts.what: Metadata describing a Pi theme discovered on disk.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Theme name (file basename without the extension).

properties.name: tier
properties.description: Tier at which this entry lives.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Absolute on-disk path to the theme file.

properties.name: fileExt
properties.description: File extension of the theme file (e.g. `".ts"`, `".json"`).

properties.name: shadowed
properties.description: See `ExtensionEntry.shadowed`.

concepts.name: HarnessInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by the Pi install verbs (extensions, prompts, themes).

properties.name: force
properties.description: When `true`, overwrite an existing file at the target tier. When `false` (the default) the install verb throws if the target exists.

concepts.name: CantProfileEntry
concepts.what: Metadata describing a `.cant` profile installed at one of the three Pi harness tiers.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Profile name (basename of the `.cant` file without the extension).

properties.name: tier
properties.description: Tier at which this entry lives.

properties.name: sourcePath
properties.description: Absolute on-disk path to the `.cant` file.

properties.name: counts
properties.description: Parsed section counts for the profile.

properties.name: shadowedByHigherTier
properties.description: When `true`, this entry is shadowed by a higher-precedence entry with the same name. Mirrors the shadow flag emitted by `Harness.listExtensions` so the same UI logic applies.

concepts.name: ValidateCantProfileResult
concepts.what: Result of validating a `.cant` profile via the cant-core 42-rule engine.

properties.name: valid
properties.description: Whether validation passed (no error-severity diagnostics).

properties.name: errors
properties.description: All diagnostics emitted by the 42-rule engine, in source order.

properties.name: counts
properties.description: Section counts for the profile (zero when parsing failed).

concepts.name: PiModelsConfig
concepts.what: Entire `models.json` document shape used by Pi.

properties.name: providers
properties.description: Map of provider id → provider block.

concepts.name: ModelListEntry
concepts.what: A model entry as surfaced by `Harness.listModels`.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: Provider id (e.g. `"anthropic"`).

properties.name: id
properties.description: Model id within the provider.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Human-readable name, from `models.json` when available.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: `true` when the model is present in `settings.json:enabledModels`.

properties.name: isDefault
properties.description: `true` when the model is the configured default.

properties.name: custom
properties.description: `true` when this model is defined in `models.json` (custom). When `false`, the entry originates from `settings.json:enabledModels` only and is assumed to resolve against Pi's built-in registry.

concepts.name: SubagentSpawnOptions
concepts.what: Per-call options that override harness-wide spawn defaults.

properties.name: onStream
properties.description: Streaming callback invoked once per parsed event from the child.

properties.name: terminateGraceMs
properties.description: Override the SIGTERM grace window before SIGKILL fires.

properties.name: env
properties.description: Environment variable overrides layered atop the task-level env.

properties.name: cwd
properties.description: Working directory override that wins over `SubagentTask.cwd`.

concepts.name: SubagentStreamEvent
concepts.what: One streaming event surfaced through `SubagentSpawnOptions.onStream`.

properties.name: kind
properties.description: Event kind discriminator.

properties.name: subagentId
properties.description: Subagent identifier (matches `SubagentHandle.subagentId`).

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description: 1-based line number within the child's stdout stream. Only set for `"message"` events that originated from a parsed stdout line.

properties.name: payload
properties.description: Event payload, shaped according to `kind`.

concepts.name: SubagentExitResult
concepts.what: Resolution value of `SubagentHandle.exitPromise`.

properties.name: code
properties.description: Process exit code, or `null` when the child was terminated by a signal before exiting normally.

properties.name: signal
properties.description: Terminating signal, or `null` when the child exited normally.

properties.name: childSessionPath
properties.description: Absolute path to the child session JSONL file on disk.

properties.name: durationMs
properties.description: Wall-clock duration from spawn to exit, in milliseconds.

concepts.name: SubagentLinkEntry
concepts.what: `subagent_link` custom entry written into the parent session JSONL.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Entry type discriminator (always `"subagent_link"`).

properties.name: subagentId
properties.description: Subagent identifier matching `SubagentHandle.subagentId`.

properties.name: taskId
properties.description: Task identifier from `SubagentTask.taskId`.

properties.name: childSessionPath
properties.description: Absolute path to the child session JSONL file.

properties.name: startedAt
properties.description: ISO-8601 timestamp captured when the child was spawned.

concepts.name: SubagentResult
concepts.what: Final result of a subagent's execution (legacy v1 shape).

properties.name: exitCode
properties.description: Process exit code, or `null` if the process was killed by a signal.

properties.name: stdout
properties.description: Full stdout captured from the subagent.

properties.name: stderr
properties.description: Full stderr captured from the subagent.

properties.name: parsed
properties.description: Parsed JSON output, when the target supports a JSON output mode and emitted well-formed JSON on stdout.

concepts.name: FormatOptions
concepts.what: Format resolution options.

properties.name: jsonFlag
properties.description: Whether `--json` was explicitly passed.

properties.name: humanFlag
properties.description: Whether `--human` was explicitly passed.

properties.name: projectDefault
properties.description: Project-level default format when no flag is given.

concepts.name: LAFSCommandOptions
concepts.what: Standard command options interface for LAFS-compliant commands.

properties.name: json
properties.description: Whether to force JSON output.

properties.name: human
properties.description: Whether to force human-readable output.

concepts.name: ProviderTargetOptions
concepts.what: Options for resolving which providers to target in advanced commands.

properties.name: all
properties.description: When true, target all registry providers including undetected ones.

properties.name: agent
properties.description: Specific provider IDs or aliases to target.

concepts.name: McpDetectionEntry
concepts.what: Result of a single provider's MCP installation probe.

properties.name: providerId
properties.description: Provider id (e.g. `"claude-code"`).

properties.name: providerName
properties.description: Human-readable provider name.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Resolved scope of the probed config file.

properties.name: configPath
properties.description: Absolute path to the provider's MCP config file.

properties.name: exists
properties.description: Whether the config file exists on disk.

properties.name: serverCount
properties.description: Number of server entries found, or `null` when the file is missing/unparseable.

properties.name: lastModified
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the file's last modification, or `null` when the file is missing.

concepts.name: InstallMcpServerOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `installMcpServer`.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope to write to (project|global).

properties.name: force
properties.description: When `true`, overwrite an existing server entry instead of failing.

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project directory used for the `project` scope.

concepts.name: InstallMcpServerResult
concepts.what: Result of an `installMcpServer` call.

properties.name: installed
properties.description: Whether the entry was written to the config file.

properties.name: conflicted
properties.description: Whether the target server name already existed before the call.

properties.name: sourcePath
properties.description: Absolute path to the config file that was (or would have been) written.

properties.name: providerId
properties.description: Provider id the entry was written for.

properties.name: serverName
properties.description: Server name that was written.

concepts.name: RemoveMcpServerOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `removeMcpServer` and `removeMcpServerFromAll`.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: Scope to target (project|global).

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project directory used for the `project` scope.

concepts.name: RemoveMcpServerResult
concepts.what: Result of a single-provider `removeMcpServer` call.

properties.name: providerId
properties.description: Provider id the call targeted.

properties.name: serverName
properties.description: Server name the call targeted.

properties.name: sourcePath
properties.description: Resolved config file path, or `null` when the provider had no MCP capability.

properties.name: removed
properties.description: Whether an entry was actually deleted.

properties.name: reason
properties.description: Diagnostic discriminator when `removed` is `false`.  - `"no-mcp-capability"` — provider does not consume MCP servers - `"no-config-path"` — provider has no config path for the scope - `"file-missing"` — config file does not exist on disk - `"entry-missing"` — config file exists but had no matching entry  Set to `null` when `removed` is `true`.

concepts.name: McpCommandBaseOptions
concepts.what: Standard option shape accepted by every `caamp mcp <verb>` command.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: `--scope project|global` (default: project).

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: `--project-dir <path>` — override cwd for the `project` scope.

concepts.name: McpDetectOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp mcp detect`.

properties.name: onlyExisting
properties.description: Show only providers that actually have a config file on disk.

concepts.name: McpInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp mcp install`.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: Provider id to install into (required).

properties.name: from
properties.description: Optional path to a JSON file containing an `McpServerConfig`.

properties.name: env
properties.description: Repeatable `KEY=VALUE` env assignments.

properties.name: force
properties.description: Overwrite an existing entry instead of failing.

concepts.name: McpListOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp mcp list`.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: Restrict the listing to a single provider id.

concepts.name: McpRemoveOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp mcp remove`.

properties.name: provider
properties.description: Provider id to remove from (mutually exclusive with --all-providers).

properties.name: allProviders
properties.description: Remove from every MCP-capable provider in the registry.

concepts.name: GitFetchResult
concepts.what: Result of fetching a Git repository to a local temporary directory.

properties.name: localPath
properties.description: Absolute path to the fetched content on disk.

properties.name: cleanup
properties.description: Cleanup function that removes the temporary directory.

concepts.name: PiCommandBaseOptions
concepts.what: Standard option shape accepted by every `caamp pi <verb>` command.

properties.name: scope
properties.description: `--scope project|user|global`.

properties.name: force
properties.description: `--force` — overwrite existing targets on install verbs.

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: `--project-dir <path>` — override cwd for the `project` tier.

concepts.name: PiCantListOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi cant list`.

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project directory used for the `project` tier.

concepts.name: PiCantInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi cant install`.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Profile name override. Defaults to the inferred source name.

concepts.name: PiCantRemoveOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi cant remove`.

concepts.name: PiExtensionsListOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi extensions list`.

properties.name: projectDir
properties.description: Project directory used for the `project` tier.

concepts.name: PiExtensionsInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi extensions install`.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Extension name override. Defaults to the inferred source name.

concepts.name: PiExtensionsRemoveOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi extensions remove`.

concepts.name: PiModelsCommandOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by every `caamp pi models` verb.

properties.name: global
properties.description: `--global` targets the Pi global state root instead of the project.

concepts.name: PiModelsAddOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi models add`.

properties.name: displayName
properties.description: Human-readable model name.

properties.name: baseUrl
properties.description: Override the provider base URL.

properties.name: reasoning
properties.description: Reasoning-capable flag.

properties.name: contextWindow
properties.description: Context window size in tokens.

properties.name: maxTokens
properties.description: Maximum output tokens.

concepts.name: PiPromptsInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi prompts install`.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Override the inferred prompt directory name.

concepts.name: PiPromptsListOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi prompts list`.

concepts.name: PiPromptsRemoveOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi prompts remove`.

concepts.name: PiSessionsListOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi sessions list`.

properties.name: includeSubagents
properties.description: Include sessions under the `subagents/` subdirectory (default: true).

concepts.name: PiSessionsExportOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi sessions export`.

properties.name: jsonl
properties.description: Emit raw JSONL. Mutually exclusive with `md`.

properties.name: md
properties.description: Emit Markdown (filtered to message/custom_message entries).

properties.name: output
properties.description: Write to this file path instead of stdout.

concepts.name: PiThemesInstallOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi themes install`.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Override the inferred theme name.

concepts.name: PiThemesListOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi themes list`.

concepts.name: PiThemesRemoveOptions
concepts.what: Options accepted by `caamp pi themes remove`.

concepts.name: EventSource
concepts.what: Union type of valid event source types.

concepts.name: CanonicalHookEvent
concepts.what: Union type of all canonical hook event names.

concepts.name: CanonicalEventDefinition
concepts.what: Definition of a canonical hook event including its category and behavior.

properties.name: category
properties.description: The lifecycle category this event belongs to (e.g. `"session"`, `"tool"`).

properties.name: description
properties.description: Human-readable description of when this event fires.

properties.name: canBlock
properties.description: Whether a hook handler can block or cancel the associated action.

concepts.name: NormalizedHookEvent
concepts.what: A fully resolved hook event with both canonical and native names.

properties.name: canonical
properties.description: The CAAMP canonical event name.

properties.name: native
properties.description: The provider-native event name.

properties.name: providerId
properties.description: The provider this event was resolved for.

properties.name: category
properties.description: The lifecycle category of this event.

properties.name: canBlock
properties.description: Whether a handler for this event can block the associated action.

concepts.name: HookSupportResult
concepts.what: Result of querying whether a provider supports a specific canonical event.

properties.name: canonical
properties.description: The canonical event that was queried.

properties.name: supported
properties.description: Whether the provider supports this event.

properties.name: native
properties.description: The provider-native event name, or `null` if unsupported.

properties.name: notes
properties.description: Optional notes about support caveats.

concepts.name: MarketplaceResult
concepts.what: Normalized marketplace record returned by all adapters.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Short skill name (e.g. `"memory"`).

properties.name: scopedName
properties.description: Scoped name including author prefix (e.g. `"\@anthropic/memory"`).

properties.name: description
properties.description: Short description of what the skill does.

properties.name: author
properties.description: Author or organization name.

properties.name: stars
properties.description: GitHub star count.

properties.name: githubUrl
properties.description: Full GitHub repository URL.

properties.name: repoFullName
properties.description: GitHub `owner/repo` path.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Path within the repository to the skill file.

properties.name: source
properties.description: Name of the marketplace source this result came from.

concepts.name: SearchOptions
concepts.what: Options for marketplace search requests.

properties.name: query
properties.description: Free-text search query.

properties.name: limit
properties.description: Maximum number of results.

properties.name: offset
properties.description: Pagination offset.

properties.name: sortBy
properties.description: Sort order for results.

properties.name: category
properties.description: Filter by skill category.

properties.name: author
properties.description: Filter by author name.

concepts.name: RecommendationValidationResult

concepts.name: RecommendationOptions
concepts.what: Configuration options for the skill recommendation engine.

properties.name: top
properties.description: Maximum number of results to return from the ranked list.

properties.name: includeDetails
properties.description: Whether to include detailed score breakdown in each result.

properties.name: weights
properties.description: Partial weight overrides for individual scoring factors.

properties.name: modernMarkers
properties.description: Custom modern technology marker strings for modernity scoring.

properties.name: legacyMarkers
properties.description: Custom legacy technology marker strings for modernity scoring.

concepts.name: RecommendSkillsResult
concepts.what: The complete result of a skill recommendation operation.

properties.name: criteria
properties.description: The normalized criteria used for scoring.

properties.name: ranking
properties.description: Skills ranked by recommendation score, highest first.

concepts.name: SearchSkillsOptions
concepts.what: Options for searching skills via marketplace APIs.

properties.name: limit
properties.description: Maximum number of results to return.

concepts.name: RecommendSkillsQueryOptions
concepts.what: Options for the recommendation query combining ranking options with a result limit.

properties.name: limit
properties.description: Maximum number of results to return.

concepts.name: ValidationResult
concepts.what: Result of validating a SKILL.md file against the Agent Skills standard.

properties.name: valid
properties.description: Whether the skill passed validation (no error-level issues).

properties.name: issues
properties.description: All issues found during validation.

properties.name: metadata
properties.description: Parsed frontmatter metadata, or `null` if parsing failed.

concepts.name: SpawnOptions
concepts.what: Options for spawning a subagent.

properties.name: prompt
properties.description: The prompt or instruction to give the spawned agent.

properties.name: model
properties.description: Model to use for the spawned agent.

properties.name: tools
properties.description: Tools to make available to the spawned agent.

properties.name: timeout
properties.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the spawned agent.

properties.name: isolate
properties.description: Whether to isolate the spawned agent (e.g. in a worktree).

concepts.name: SpawnResult
concepts.what: Result from a spawn operation.

properties.name: instanceId
properties.description: Unique identifier for the spawned agent instance.

properties.name: status
properties.description: Current status of the spawned agent.

properties.name: output
properties.description: Output produced by the spawned agent.

concepts.name: SkillIntegrityStatus

properties.name: name

properties.name: status
properties.description: Overall integrity status.

properties.name: canonicalExists

properties.name: canonicalPath

properties.name: linkStatuses

type.original: { providerId: string; linkPath: string; exists: boolean; isSymlink: boolean; pointsToCanonical: boolean; }[]

operations.name: buildSkillSubPathCandidates
operations.what: Builds a list of candidate sub-paths for skill file resolution.

inputs.name: marketplacePath
inputs.description: The sub-path from the marketplace listing

inputs.name: parsedPath
inputs.description: The sub-path parsed from the source URL

type.original: A deduplicated array of candidate sub-paths

outputs.description: A deduplicated array of candidate sub-paths

operations.name: buildSkillsMap
operations.what: Build a full skills map for all providers.

type.original: Array of skills map entries with provider ID, tool name, precedence, and paths

outputs.description: Array of skills map entries with provider ID, tool name, precedence, and paths

operations.name: buildInjectionContent
operations.what: Build injection content from a structured template.  Produces a string suitable for injection between CAAMP markers. References are output as `@` lines, content blocks are appended as-is.

inputs.name: template
inputs.description: Template defining references and content

operations.name: generateInjectionContent
operations.what: Generate a standard CAAMP injection block for instruction files.  Produces markdown content suitable for injection between CAAMP markers. Optionally includes MCP server and custom content sections.

inputs.name: options
inputs.description: Optional configuration for the generated content

operations.name: generateSkillsSection
operations.what: Generate a skills discovery section for instruction files.

inputs.name: skillNames
inputs.description: Array of skill names to list

operations.name: checkInjection
operations.what: Check the status of a CAAMP injection block in an instruction file.  Returns the injection status: - `"missing"` - File does not exist - `"none"` - File exists but has no CAAMP markers - `"current"` - CAAMP block exists and matches expected content (or no expected content given) - `"outdated"` - CAAMP block exists but differs from expected content

inputs.name: filePath
inputs.description: Absolute path to the instruction file

inputs.name: expectedContent
inputs.description: Optional expected content to compare against

operations.name: checkAllInjections
operations.what: Check injection status across all providers' instruction files.  Deduplicates by file path since multiple providers may share the same instruction file (e.g. many providers use `AGENTS.md`).

inputs.name: providers
inputs.description: Array of providers to check

inputs.name: projectDir
inputs.description: Absolute path to the project directory

inputs.name: scope
inputs.description: Whether to check project or global instruction files

inputs.name: expectedContent
inputs.description: Optional expected content to compare against

type.original: Array of injection check results, one per unique instruction file

outputs.description: Array of injection check results, one per unique instruction file

operations.name: buildEnvelope
operations.what: Builds a standard LAFS envelope.

inputs.name: operation
inputs.description: Operation identifier (e.g., `"skills.list"`, `"doctor.check"`)

inputs.name: mvi
inputs.description: Machine-Verified Instruction disclosure level

inputs.name: result

inputs.name: error
inputs.description: Error details (`null` if success)

inputs.name: page
inputs.description: Pagination info (`null` if not applicable)

inputs.name: sessionId
inputs.description: Optional session identifier

inputs.name: warnings
inputs.description: Optional array of warnings to attach

operations.name: runLafsCommand
operations.what: Runs an async action and emits the result as a LAFS success or error envelope.

inputs.name: command
inputs.description: The LAFS operation identifier

inputs.name: mvi
inputs.description: The minimum viable information level

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: The async function to execute

constraints.rule: Error if `providers/registry.json` cannot be found within 8 parent levels
Error if the provider ID is not found in the registry
Error if any provider ID is not found in the registry
`Error` when `tier='project'` and no `projectDir` is supplied
`PiRequiredError` when mode is `'force-pi'` and Pi is not   installed.
Error if format flags conflict
If the file cannot be read or the format is unsupported
If the format is unsupported
If the format is unsupported
`Error` when the provider has no MCP capability or no   project-scoped config path is available.
`LAFSCommandError` when the provider is unknown or has no   MCP capability.
`LAFSCommandError` when `raw` is set to an invalid value.
`LAFSCommandError` when the token is malformed.
`NetworkError` on timeout or network failure
`NetworkError` when `response.ok` is `false`
`LAFSCommandError` when Pi is not installed.
Error with `code` and `issues` properties when criteria are invalid
If the module cannot be loaded or does not implement SkillLibrary
If skills.json is not found at the root
Error if the library cannot be loaded from the given path

configSchema.description: Detection methods to try, in order.
Binary name to look up on PATH (for `"binary"` method).
Directories to check for existence (for `"directory"` method).
macOS .app bundle name (for `"appBundle"` method).
Flatpak application ID (for `"flatpak"` method).
Transport type (`"stdio"`, `"sse"`, or `"http"`).
URL for remote MCP servers.
HTTP headers for remote MCP servers.
Command to run for stdio MCP servers.
Arguments for the stdio command.
Environment variables for the stdio process.
Target agent IDs (repeatable).
Operate on global config instead of project.
Skip confirmation prompts.
Target all detected agents.
Output as JSON.
Preview changes without writing.
Enable debug logging.
Suppress non-error output.
`\@` references to inject (e.g. `["\@AGENTS.md"]`).
Optional inline content blocks.
Whether this is a global or project-level file.
Whether to bypass the cache and force a fresh detection scan.
Time-to-live for cached results in milliseconds.
Explicit list of providers to target, auto-detected if omitted.
Minimum provider priority threshold for filtering.
Skill operations to apply in the batch.
Project root directory, defaults to `process.cwd()`.
Tier to resolve.
Asset kind (extensions, prompts, themes, sessions, cant).

Project directory used when `tier` is `project`. Ignored for other tiers. When omitted with `tier='project'` the caller MUST substitute `process.cwd()` before invoking the resolver, so the harness never silently resolves to an unexpected working directory.
Explicit list of providers requested by the user (e.g. via `--agent`).
configSchema.effect: When supplied, this list signals that the caller is honouring an explicit user selection rather than asking for the implicit default. In `auto` mode, an explicit list that excludes Pi while Pi is installed triggers a one-time deprecation warning per process. In `force-pi` mode the explicit list is ignored if it does not contain Pi (Pi is still required). In `legacy` mode the list is returned verbatim with no warning.  Pass `undefined` (or omit entirely) to request the implicit default resolution.

configSchema.description: When `true`, overwrite an existing file at the target tier. When `false` (the default) the install verb throws if the target exists.
Map of provider id → provider block.
Streaming callback invoked once per parsed event from the child.
configSchema.effect: The harness fires this for every line of stdout (parsed as JSON when possible), every line of stderr, the final exit, and the `subagent_link` write. Callbacks are best-effort: throwing from the callback is caught and recorded as a stderr line so the spawn loop is never aborted by user code.

configSchema.description: Override the SIGTERM grace window before SIGKILL fires.
configSchema.effect: When omitted, the harness reads `settings.json:pi.subagent.terminateGraceMs` (global scope) and falls back to `5000` ms if absent or invalid. Tests use very small values to keep cleanup checks fast.

configSchema.description: Environment variable overrides layered atop the task-level env.
configSchema.effect: Convenience hook for per-call secrets that should not live on the task object itself. Merged after `SubagentTask.env` so call-site keys win.

configSchema.description: Working directory override that wins over `SubagentTask.cwd`.
configSchema.effect: Useful when the same task description is reused across multiple working directories.

configSchema.description: Whether `--json` was explicitly passed.
Whether `--human` was explicitly passed.
Project-level default format when no flag is given.
Whether to force JSON output.
Whether to force human-readable output.
When true, target all registry providers including undetected ones.
Specific provider IDs or aliases to target.
Scope to write to (project|global).
When `true`, overwrite an existing server entry instead of failing.
Project directory used for the `project` scope.
Scope to target (project|global).
Project directory used for the `project` scope.
`--scope project|global` (default: project).
`--project-dir <path>` — override cwd for the `project` scope.
Show only providers that actually have a config file on disk.
Provider id to install into (required).
Optional path to a JSON file containing an `McpServerConfig`.
Repeatable `KEY=VALUE` env assignments.
Overwrite an existing entry instead of failing.
Restrict the listing to a single provider id.
Provider id to remove from (mutually exclusive with --all-providers).
Remove from every MCP-capable provider in the registry.
`--scope project|user|global`.
`--force` — overwrite existing targets on install verbs.
`--project-dir <path>` — override cwd for the `project` tier.
Project directory used for the `project` tier.
Profile name override. Defaults to the inferred source name.
Project directory used for the `project` tier.
Extension name override. Defaults to the inferred source name.
`--global` targets the Pi global state root instead of the project.
Human-readable model name.
Override the provider base URL.
Reasoning-capable flag.
Context window size in tokens.
Maximum output tokens.
Override the inferred prompt directory name.
Include sessions under the `subagents/` subdirectory (default: true).
Emit raw JSONL. Mutually exclusive with `md`.
Emit Markdown (filtered to message/custom_message entries).
Write to this file path instead of stdout.
Override the inferred theme name.
Free-text search query.
Maximum number of results.
Pagination offset.
Sort order for results.
Filter by skill category.
Filter by author name.
Maximum number of results to return from the ranked list.
Whether to include detailed score breakdown in each result.
Partial weight overrides for individual scoring factors.
Custom modern technology marker strings for modernity scoring.
Custom legacy technology marker strings for modernity scoring.
Maximum number of results to return.
Maximum number of results to return.
The prompt or instruction to give the spawned agent.
Model to use for the spawned agent.
Tools to make available to the spawned agent.
Timeout in milliseconds for the spawned agent.
Whether to isolate the spawned agent (e.g. in a worktree).
